scenarios.name: Normal Day
scenarios.description: Typical Ontario winter weekday. No forecast surprises, no derating. Good default for a first run.

scenarios.name: Overnight Wind Surplus
scenarios.description: Strong overnight wind pushes prices toward zero from midnight to 05:00. Rewards charging during the surplus window.

scenarios.name: Evening Demand Peak
scenarios.description: System demand and price both spike harder than the normal-day baseline between 17:00 and 20:00.

scenarios.name: Demand Forecast Increase
scenarios.description: The planner sees the normal-day demand forecast. Actual system demand comes in materially higher; used to test plan robustness.
scenarios.hiddenStressDescription: Actual demand across the horizon comes in 12% above the forecast that was used for planning.

scenarios.name: Battery Derating
scenarios.description: A known thermal restriction reduces charge and discharge capability by 30% during the 17:00-20:00 peak window. This is announced ahead of planning.

scenarios.name: Renewable Forecast Miss
scenarios.description: The planner sees an optimistic overnight wind forecast. Actual overnight wind output undershoots that forecast by half; used to test plan robustness.
scenarios.hiddenStressDescription: Overnight wind output (00:00-05:00) actually reaches only 50% of the forecast used for planning.

scenarios.name: Combined Adversarial Event
scenarios.description: The announced peak-hour derating from the Battery Derating scenario, combined with an overnight wind shortfall and an evening demand surprise. The hardest scenario in the catalog.
scenarios.hiddenStressDescription: Overnight wind undershoots forecast by 50% and evening demand (16:00-20:00) comes in 12% above forecast, layered on top of the announced peak-hour derating.
